The Content Creator Challenge (CCC) is a hands-on course that students take in
their second year to learn the art and business of content creation. In today’s
digital economy where attention is currency and influence drives buying decisions,
CCC gives students a sharp edge in building personal brands and mastering
platform-first strategies.
Course Overview
Students receive platform-specific training across Instagram, YouTube, and LinkedIn,
learning how to
Build an authentic personal bran 
Collaborate with real-world influencer 
Grow and nurture an online communit 
Craft content that engages and convert 
Understand performance metrics and monetization
Core focus of the course is influencer marketing, now central to how brands connect
with audiences. With creator-led campaigns outpacing traditional media, the ability to
influence at scale is a game-changing skill across industries like D2C, fashion, tech,
and more.

What is

Masters’ Union?
Hon’ble Vice President of India, Shri Jagdeep Dhankar at the 4th Convocation of Masters’ Union
01

[Page 5]
Where Business & Products Aren’t Just Studied

------- They’re Built
Founded in 2020, Masters’ Union was envisioned as a modern business & engineering school that stands for “Learn By Doing”, bridging the gap
between traditional education and the dynamic needs of the industry.

Students build businesses to learn business and develop tech to master tech. Grades are based on real products, revenue, profits, and NPS—not
just exams.

Unlike conventional institutions, Masters’ Union is led by industry practitioners—CEOs, founders, technocrats and business leaders—who design
and teach its innovative, hands-on curriculum.

In just four years, it has grown from a single batch of 60 students to a thriving institution with over 2,500 students, producing groundbreaking
startups, winning national case competitions, and setting new benchmarks in placements.
02

A Unique InClass-OutClass System
Transform ‘InClass’ theory into immediate ‘OutClass’ practice.
InClass is where the magic begins, with interactive, hands-on sessions led by

into the classroom.
With OutClass, students apply what they learn inside the class and dive into live
projects with top companies, collaborate with startups, and even launch their own
ventures!

High School

------- Startup League
The High School Startup League is India’s largest platform for teen entrepreneurs—often called the
Shark Tank for high schoolers. Students pitch their ventures to top VCs and angel investors, raising
₹20–50 lakhs in real funding. From Shantanu Deshpande to Arjun Vaidya, the country’s most
respected founders and investors mentor and judge the finalists. With millions of views and nationwide
participation, it’s become a flagship event that inspires, funds, and launches the next generation of
Indian entrepreneurs.

# PGP APPLIED AI AND AGENTIC SYSTEMS - OFFICIAL PROGRAM DETAILS

- Campus Location: Gurugram, India (DLF Cyberpark).
- Total Program Fee: ₹22,65,000.
- Program Duration: 15 months (divided into 6 terms).
- Next Batch Starts: August 2026.
- Program Modes: 
  - Offline (On-Campus) for freshers or early career engineers.
  - Online Weekend Programme for working professionals to apply AI at their current job.

### ADMISSION PROCESS (4 STEPS)
1. Application Submission: Online academic records and professional experience.
2. Scholastic Technical Test: MU-BAAT or Scholastic Technical Test (GMAT/GRE/CAT also accepted).
3. Technical Interview: Interviewed by faculty and industry experts/mentors.
4. Admission Decision: Final decision via offer letter.

### FINANCES & INSTALLMENTS
- Total Program Fee: ₹22,65,000 (Inclusive of all tuition).
- Admission Offer Acceptance Fee: ₹75,000 (Non-refundable).
- Mid-Programme Installment: ₹10,95,000 (Due 3 months after program start).
- Scholarship: Merit-based and profile-based scholarships of up to 100% are available.
- EMI Option: Available via partner banks (HDFC, Bajaj Finserv). 12-month EMI is exactly ₹188,750 per month.

### CAREER OUTCOMES
- Domestic Placement Average: ₹30.72 LPA.
- Overall Average Package: ₹33.39 LPA.
- Highest International Package: ₹1.28 Cr.
- Highest Domestic Package: ₹33.39 LPA.
- Top Recruiters: Google, Meta, Apple, Microsoft, Amazon.
- Student Startups: Alumni have founded startups like MEMOTAG, VKYD Labs, TheGoodBrowser, and GuarderX. Some were featured on Shark Tank India.

### CURRICULUM HIGHLIGHTS
- Term 1: Python for AI, Linear Algebra, Probability.
- Term 2: Machine Learning Deep Dive, Supervised/Unsupervised Learning.
- Term 3: LLMs and Transformers, Prompt Engineering.
- Term 4: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), Neural Networks, and Data Engineering.
- Term 5: Agentic Workflows, LangGraph, AutoGen.
- Term 6: Capstone Project & Deployment.
course: PGP Applied AI and Agentic Systems

### FACULTY & MENTORS
- Divij Bajaj: Data and Applied Scientist at Microsoft (Lead Faculty/Mentor).
- Faculty Qualifications: 100% of lead faculty hold a PhD doctorate from globally renowned universities.

### ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA
- Offline Program: Best for BCA, B.Tech, or Computer Science related degree holders; freshers or early career professionals.
- Online Weekend Program: Designed for working professionals with any degree background seeking to integrate AI.

### CONTACT INFORMATION
- Admissions Inquiry: +91 8929361765.
- Email: pgadmissions@mastersunion.org.

### MISCELLANEOUS FAQ
- Hostel WiFi Speed: "I don't have that detail. Please contact pgadmissions@mastersunion.org."
- Marketing/HR Modules: "There are no marketing or HR modules in the Applied AI context; this program is 100% technical."

Enrollment Checklist
Admissions Criteria
Academic Background
We welcome candidates from diverse educational
backgrounds. Whether you’ve completed formal education or have gained knowledge
through experience, we value a strong passion for learning and personal growth.
Financial Acumen
While financial knowledge is helpful, it’s not
a requirement. If you have a curiosity about finance and are eager to build your
skills, the CMT Programme is here to help you grow and develop in this area.
Critical Thinking
We look for individuals who can approach
problems with clarity, curiosity, and sound judgement. If you enjoy analysing
challenges and making thoughtful decisions, you’ll thrive in the CMT Programme.

Personal Details
Professional Details
Online
Interview
Requirements
If shortlisted, applicants will be invited for an
online interview, which will help us get to know them better, and
help them understand all aspects of PGP in Capital Markets &
Trading. The interview panel will consist of our eminent Masters
from academia and the industry, as well as members of the core team.
Final Admission
Decision
Once the Admissions Office receives feedback from
the panel, the application will be re-evaluated and then presented
to the Admissions Committee, which makes the final decision for each
candidate. Applicants will be advised of the final decision on their
application through an email.
The entire admissions process, from the
Application Deadline, will take up to 4 weeks.

Frequently Asked
Questions
How do I decide which programme to choose, or which programme is right
for me?
If you are dealing with complex family dynamics or need
long-term business transformation, leadership growth, and legacy building, OPM is the right
choice. It is a 12-month programme for those focused on sustainable growth, succession, and
global business exposure.
If there are not too many complications in your family dynamics, your primary pain point is
sales or business acceleration, and you want to make a shorter commitment of your time, then
9-month BAP is a better program for you.
What is the main outcome difference?
12 months OPM = holistic, sustainable transformation and leadership

sales or business acceleration, and you want to make a shorter commitment of your time, then
9-month BAP is a better program for you.
What is the main outcome difference?
12 months OPM = holistic, sustainable transformation and leadership
9 months BAP = fast, practical sales and process gains
Will there be the same level of rigour of practical application in
both the programs?
Yes. Both programs include assignments and Capstone to ensure
whatever is taught in the class is thoroughly applied in your company. A pressing problem
that your organisation is currently facing is solved with the help of Capstone, with one on
one coaching by faculty and mentors.
Will I miss out a lot by choosing the BAP?
It's all about what your need is. Yes, you will miss out on
some aspects like in-depth modules for family, self, patriarch, non family CXOs. Also,
international immersions and Fractional Board. Nevertheless, if your current pressing need
is revenue growth, then you can go for BAP.
Is it worth doing the 12-month program if my goal is only sales
growth?
It is better to compare all aspects of both programs, what
different and additional you are getting in each, and then decide.
Can freshers apply for the 9-month BAP Certificate?
Not recommended. Ideal for those with at
least 2–3 years of business/operations experience.
Is the 9-month BAP program also designed for family businesses?
Both OPM and BAP are designed keeping
needs of family business owners and promoters.
Is the 9-month BAP course a subset of OPM?
No. It is a different program. While some
sessions are similar to OPM, there are many sessions in BAP which are not taught in OPM. See
Modules Comparison.
Can I switch from OPM to BAP?
OPM's first batch with generous
scholarships, offer of bringing a family member for free, international immersions,
Fractional Board, and almost 2X course modules offers incredible value. Hence, we strongly
dissuade students from switching from OPM to BAP.

Can I switch from OPM to BAP?
OPM's first batch with generous
scholarships, offer of bringing a family member for free, international immersions,
Fractional Board, and almost 2X course modules offers incredible value. Hence, we strongly
dissuade students from switching from OPM to BAP.
Having completed BAP, can I upgrade myself to OPM by doing additional
courses and modules?
Yes, it is possible by doing additional
modules and international immersions. However, we would like to understand your business
objectives and then recommend the best way forward.
Can I upgrade from BAP to OPM during the program?
Yes, by completing additional modules and
paying incremental fees. However, the total cost of upgrading through this pathway will be
higher than pursuing OPM directly from the beginning. We recommend carefully evaluating your
long term objectives before making this decision.
Can I bring a family member or my professional team to the 9-month
BAP program?
No. This benefit is exclusively available for the first cohort of 12-month OPM.
Is the business simulation/ personal interview conducted online or
offline?
The in-person business simulation and
interview will be conducted on the same day for a student. These will be scheduled across
major cities (Delhi, Bengaluru, Mumbai, Ahmedabad, Indore, Hyderabad, Chandigarh) in India
and over Zoom in exceptional cases.
What is GD or business simulation ? How to prepare for it?
The in-person business simulation will
require students to role-play as a CXO of a company and solve its business challenges in a
team of 5-6 applicants. We will send them a case study to prepare for the business
simulation round 3-4 days prior for preparation. In this round, they will be judged on their
creativity, communication, and teamwork skills. They will be participating in a group
discussion for 45 minutes followed by a presentation (without any slides).
What happens in the personal interview, what kind of questions will be
asked?

creativity, communication, and teamwork skills. They will be participating in a group
discussion for 45 minutes followed by a presentation (without any slides).
What happens in the personal interview, what kind of questions will be
asked?
The interview will be a one-on-one
conversation with our faculty or senior leadership. The questions will be around their
application and motivations for applying to Masters' Union. The goal is to know more about
students and what they want to achieve at Masters' Union.
Is there any detailed information about GD/PI ?
The overall process will take around 3 hours.
The business simulation will last 45 minutes followed by a 15 minute group
presentation. Students will be working in teams of 4-6 students on a
simulated
business scenario, where students will be expected to make decisions,
analyse
data and present their findings.
The interview will be conducted by a senior leader or a faculty member at
Masters’ Union and will be approximately 30 minutes long. They will be asked
questions about their application including their achievements, essays and
video essay submitted.
Dress code for the event is smart casuals.
When will the results be declared?
The results are announced 30-45 days after
the date of the interview. You can find more details
here
Is there a firm deadline for payment of the admission fee?
We provide students ample time till July
of the academic year so that they can make their decision based on all the admission offers
that they have. In case they still want an extension, the same can be granted based on an
email request.
What is the mode of payment for the fees?
Mode of payment can be online, net
banking, bank transfer or in some rare cases they can even send a cheque. Except for cash,
we accept all payment methods.
What is the fee payment schedule?
Students can pay the fees semester wise.
Starting every semester, they will be notified about the fees due by
ugadmissions@mastersunion.org

banking, bank transfer or in some rare cases they can even send a cheque. Except for cash,
we accept all payment methods.
What is the fee payment schedule?
Students can pay the fees semester wise.
Starting every semester, they will be notified about the fees due by
ugadmissions@mastersunion.org
Is the study abroad programme included in the fee? Is there a
different fee?
The study abroad programme is not included
in the standard tuition fee. Students opting for the 3+1 Dual Degree UG Programme will need
to pay tuition fee as per Illinois Tech & living expenses of Chicago.
Does Masters’ Union offer scholarships?
At Masters’ Union we believe that financial constraints should
not pose any hindrance to students’ academic aspirations. We have merit and need-based
scholarships for all deserving candidates. Students scoring above 35 out of 45 marks in the
Masters’ Union Scholarship Aptitude test get a scholarship which can range from 10% - 25% of
the tuition fee depending on the student’s performance in the Scholarship (Aptitude)Test and
the overall profile of the applicant. For candidates keen on scholarship, we encourage
students to submit their application early to allow for our admission counsellors to
thoroughly review their scholarship case before presenting it to the admissions committee at
the time of evaluation.
Is the scholarship for all four years or just for the first year?
Scholarship is awarded for all four years. If a student
receives a 20% scholarship, they will receive a 20% scholarship every semester on their
respective fees. However, in cases of misconduct or rule violations, disciplinary action may
be taken.
Is the Masters’ Union scholarship applicable to the Illinois Tech fee?
No, the Masters’ Union scholarship is not applicable to the
Illinois Tech fee. However, students nominated and admitted to Illinois Tech through the 3+1
Dual Degree UG Programme may be eligible for a merit-based scholarship of up to 25% of
tuition fees.

No, the Masters’ Union scholarship is not applicable to the
Illinois Tech fee. However, students nominated and admitted to Illinois Tech through the 3+1
Dual Degree UG Programme may be eligible for a merit-based scholarship of up to 25% of
tuition fees.
To qualify, students must maintain a cumulative GPA of 3.0/4.0 or higher. The final
decision on scholarship awards and eligibility is determined by Illinois Tech in accordance
with its admission and scholarship policies.
Are there any financing options?
Yes, there are financial institutions that can provide loans
for up to 95% of the programme fees to the admitted students. Read
for further
details.
Is the admissions process different for the 3+1 Dual Degree UG
Programme with Illinois Institute of Technology?
No, the admissions process for the 3+1 Dual Degree UG
Programme is the same as the standard process. Applicants must apply through the regular
admissions portal, and eligibility for the programme is assessed based on their academic
performance, extracurricular activities, and an interview.
Will students have the option to choose the duration of transfer?
Currently, the programme is designed as a one-year transfer
option. Students will spend their fourth year at Illinois Institute of Technology,
completing the final year of their undergraduate degree as part of the 3+1 Dual Degree UG
Programme.
When can the student decide if they want to opt for the 3+1 Dual
Degree UG Programme with Illinois Institute of Technology?
Students are strongly encouraged to decide on opting for the
3+1 Dual Degree UG Programme before starting their undergraduate journey at Masters’ Union.
Early confirmation ensures guaranteed seat allocation and allows for streamlined academic
planning tailored to the programme's requirements.
While students may still choose to opt for the programme during their third year,
this is subject to seat availability, which will be communicated at that time. Confirming at

planning tailored to the programme's requirements.
While students may still choose to opt for the programme during their third year,
this is subject to seat availability, which will be communicated at that time. Confirming at
the beginning ensures a smooth transition and optimal preparation for their transfer to
Illinois Tech.
Will the curriculum at Illinois Tech align with what students study at
Masters’ Union?
Yes, the curriculum has been designed to ensure a seamless
transfer between the institutions. Students will continue to build on the foundational and
advanced topics they study at Masters’ Union.
Can students secure internships in the US while at Illinois Tech?
Yes, students will have access to Illinois Tech’s career
services, which include internship placements, job fairs, and networking events with global
employers.
What is the structure of the IIT programme?
The IIT programme of Masters' Union's UG Programme in Data
Science and Artificial Intelligence gives the student an option to enrol in IIT Guwahati's
online Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence
Check Programme
What are the benefits of this programme?
In this programme you gain a comprehensive education in data
science and artificial intelligence from two esteemed institutions, Masters’ Union and IIT
Guwahati. Along with Masters’ Union, students get access to IIT’s industry-oriented
curriculum, covering advanced topics and providing hands-on experience with high-performance
computing facilities.
What is the fee structure and who pays for it?
The fee structure for the programme is available on our
website
. Students
pay a single fee to Masters' Union for the entire 4-year Undergraduate Programme in Data
Science & AI, which includes the IIT programme fees. Masters' Union also handles all
registration and administrative processes for the chosen IIT, ensuring a hassle-free
experience for students.
Why should I not enrol directly in IIT Guwahati without Masters'
Union?

Science & AI, which includes the IIT programme fees. Masters' Union also handles all
registration and administrative processes for the chosen IIT, ensuring a hassle-free
experience for students.
Why should I not enrol directly in IIT Guwahati without Masters'
Union?
Enrolling through the collaboration with
Masters' Union offers several unique benefits:
Industry Immersion:
Spend 3 Years at Masters'
Union, followed by a one-year industry immersion at top tech giants in India,
providing you with invaluable hands-on experience.
Dual Advantage & Professional Certificate:
get parallel enrollment in the Bachelor of Science at IIT Guwahati, and also
receive a Professional Certificate for the Undergraduate Programme in Data
Science & AI from Masters' Union, enhancing your credentials.
Placement Opportunities:
Masters' Union’s
strong ties with marquee recruiters like Microsoft, Google, PwC, and Krutrim
increase your chances of securing top placements.
Can I get both the Illinois Tech degree and the IIT degree?
Yes, with the option to enrol in Bachelor of Science at IIT
Guwahati, you can pursue the degree for 3 years and spend your 4th year at Illinois Tech,
Chicago, earning a second degree — a Bachelor of Science in AI. This pathway allows you to
graduate with two prestigious degrees in 4 years, along with global exposure and placement
opportunities in the US.
Alternatively, if you choose to complete all 4 years in India, you spend your final year
immersed in a hands-on industry internship, gaining practical experience with top tech
companies.

Admissions Criteria
Academic Background
When considering candidates for admission, our business school values a strong
academic foundation combined with diverse experiences. We welcome individuals
who bring unique perspectives shaped by their education, personal initiatives,
or varied pursuits, as these enrich classroom learning and foster a dynamic peer
environment.
Leadership Potential
We seek individuals who demonstrate a strong entrepreneurial spirit, leadership
potential, and a genuine commitment to creating impact. Your prior work
experience, personal projects, or other responsibilities can provide valuable
insights into these qualities.
Communication
Effective communication is essential for
success in a family business. We will assess your communication skills through
the interviews. We are looking for candidates who can articulate their thoughts
clearly, listen attentively, and build strong relationships.

Frequently Asked
Questions
Is PGP Rise: General Management equivalent to other Executive MBA Programmes offered in
the country?
The academic content and rigour of the Masters’ Union PGP Rise: General Management
are equivalent to other Executive MBA programmes. However, Indian regulations do not allow
independent institutions to grant a Master’s degree. After completion of the programme,
applicants will receive a certificate of completion of the Post Graduate Programme in
Management for Senior Professionals.

independent institutions to grant a Master’s degree. After completion of the programme,
applicants will receive a certificate of completion of the Post Graduate Programme in
Management for Senior Professionals.
Can applicants continue to work full-time while pursuing the
programme?
Yes, applicants can continue their full-time job as they
pursue PGP Rise: General Management by Masters’ Union.
What happens when applicants are not in the residency?
Their studies will continue on the weekend in a hybrid model
where they can choose to attend in-person classes at our Gurgaon campus or join in
virtually.
What will be the batch size of the class?
We will limit the batch size to a maximum of 60 students to
maximize individual attention from masters, and have an impactful teacher-student ratio.
What are the core emphasis areas of the Masters’ Union PGP Rise: General Management
curriculum?
We offer India’s first programme with industry-focused coaching at its core. Students get
expert mentorship to define their career goals. They benefit from psychometric
assessments and industry-focused coaching, all tailored to meet the following needs of
mid-career professionals:
Enhancing your professional brand
How many days of leave will an applicant require to participate in the
immersive weeks?
Applicants will require around 10 days of leave
throughout the year to participate in the campus immersion weeks, tech-trek, and leadership
retreat.
What is included in the cost of the programme?
The programme fee for the PGP Rise: General Management by Masters’ Union will cover:
Tuition fees for courses, workshops, retreats & events
All dining excursions organised by the PGP Rise: General Management team including
breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks
How much time should an applicant set aside for coursework on the
weekdays?
We will try to keep any additional coursework on weekdays to a
bare minimum. However, the time taken to prepare for classes can vary according to the

breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks
How much time should an applicant set aside for coursework on the
weekdays?
We will try to keep any additional coursework on weekdays to a
bare minimum. However, the time taken to prepare for classes can vary according to the
subject and applicants’ expertise on the same. On average applicants can expect to spend
5-10 hours a week on weekdays. There will be additional material provided to the applicants
in the form of written content, videos or podcasts that they can choose to engage with at
their convenience.
Is the PGP Rise: General Management model as valuable as a full-time residential PGP?
While the programme design differs from that of a full-time
PGP, the curriculum and structure is equivalent to that of any recognized executive MBA
globally. The structure is designed to be flexible to accommodate the busy schedules of
working professionals and the content is tailored for those with considerable work
experience.
What are the benefits of the 1-year format?
PGP Rise: General Management by Masters’ Union, with its unique 12-month weekend
format, covers best-in-class practices in global executive management education through an
accelerated pedagogical approach. Designed to offer flexibility as a working
professional so applicants don’t have to leave their job to pursue the course, the 4 terms
will cover rigorous academic training in core areas. Applicants can choose to go deeper in
the industry of their choice through industry electives and find stellar opportunities to
network with like-minded individuals through the residency weeks, a tech-trek and leadership
retreat. The dynamic format of the programme will offer accelerated learning in the skills
of time management and prioritisation, and thus equips them with stellar decision-making
facilities, which is one of the most valued qualities of a business leader in today’s times.
How many years of experience do applicants need
to be admitted?

of time management and prioritisation, and thus equips them with stellar decision-making
facilities, which is one of the most valued qualities of a business leader in today’s times.
How many years of experience do applicants need
to be admitted?
Applicants with work experience are preferred to apply for the programme. In case any applicant has lesser work experience,
feel free to reach out to the admissions team who will evaluate their profile and advise
accordingly.
Is there an age limit for entry?
There is no age limit for entry. However,
the programme is more suitable for candidates with experience of at least 5 years. If they
have less work experience, we encourage them to get in touch with the admissions team who
will evaluate their profile and advise them accordingly.
Who are we looking for?
We are looking for a candidate who
exhibits strong leadership performance and potential, makes the best use of opportunities
available, and is motivated to create an impact.
Do we need to write a GMAT, GRE, or CAT to enrol
in this programme?
No, this programme doesn’t require GMAT,
GRE, or CAT scores.
Can applicants get their profile evaluated?
Feel free to connect with the admissions
team and our counsellors to discuss fitment with the programme. Applicants are welcome to
send their CV to
and initiate a conversation with our counsellors
for the same.
Are all candidates interviewed?
Post the application process, the
shortlisted candidates are invited to an online interview with our admissions panel.
If an application has been unsuccessful, may
they reapply in the next round?
No, they cannot reapply in the next round.
Unsuccessful applicants can reapply for the next intake, next year. Re-applicants are
requested to submit a new online application unless otherwise indicated.
Please exercise judgement in deciding if they have made any significant changes in their new
profile since their first application, that would make a significant difference to their
admissions chances.

requested to submit a new online application unless otherwise indicated.
Please exercise judgement in deciding if they have made any significant changes in their new
profile since their first application, that would make a significant difference to their
admissions chances.
How strict are the application deadlines?
Our deadlines are non-negotiable.
Applications must be completed and submitted by midnight (Indian Standard Time) to be
included in the ongoing round. A complete application includes the online form, all
supporting documents, references, essays, and payment of the application fees.
How will applicants be graded for the course?
All participants will be graded through a
combination of quizzes, exams, group assignments, class engagement, projects, simulations,
reflections, etc. The faculty will decide the specific grading matrix for each course.
Can applicants tailor their learning journey at
PGP Rise: General Management?
We offer 16 electives, empowering students
to take charge of their time and construct a relevant programme that is aligned with their
interests and career goals. We have new-age, core, and industry concentrations so they can
choose to pursue a generalist path toward management or specialise in particular areas by
choosing industry concentrations. However, regardless of the path students decide to take
during their elective terms, everybody graduates with the same PGP Rise: General Management certificate with an
opportunity to choose industry concentrations.
Are any of the rounds more or less competitive
than others?
Each round is equally competitive. The
acceptance ratio is the same for all rounds. Most importantly, applicants should dedicate
enough time and thought to their application so that it accurately represents their
strengths and talents.
However, if scholarships are a priority, we encourage applicants to apply in Round 1.
How should applicants approach the essay
section?
The essay section in the application

enough time and thought to their application so that it accurately represents their
strengths and talents.
However, if scholarships are a priority, we encourage applicants to apply in Round 1.
How should applicants approach the essay
section?
The essay section in the application
section requires careful consideration and thought. We’re looking for clarity, conciseness,
and cohesion in arguments of applicants as well as in the structure of their essay. Most
importantly, we encourage them to bring out their most authentic self through these essays
to give us the best understanding of their thought process, critical thinking, and
communication skills.
Are the fees refunded in case applicants decide
to drop out of the programme?
Fees once submitted are not eligible for
refunds.
Masters’ Union reserves the right to amend this Refund Policy at any time without prior
notice.
Can applicants defer my admission offer from
Masters' Union to the next year?
Currently, we are not offering the option
to defer their admission offer. Those who cannot join the programme in the current year are
advised to apply again for the next year.

QUERIES
Frequently Asked
Questions
Why is PGP Rise: General Management (Global) a great fit for
working professionals looking
to advance their careers?
Each aspect of the programme has been designed to help
learners stay competitive in a dynamic market. The flexible format accommodates busy
schedules while the business challenges and practitioner led classes ensure learners
can immediately apply what they learn. Tailored mentorship addresses individual
growth areas ensuring leadership readiness. Ultimately, learners are primed to drive
change in their chosen profession.
How does PGP Rise: General Management (Global) ensure learners
stay ahead of industry
trends and innovations?
With 100% industry led classes, learners get
cutting-edge insight to industry practices. Immersions to global markets keep
learners at the forefront of innovation and shifts in the business landscape.
Furthermore, tailored coaching guides learners through market changes. The
programme’s dynamic learning methodology ensures learners are prepared for the
future of business.
What if an applicant cannot attend a residency?
We would urge all learners to attend the residencies
in-person, however, they can join virtually if they are unable to attend.
How much time off will an applicant need for the immersions?
Applicants will need about 11 days of leave throughout the course of the
programme.
Who are we looking for?
We are looking for executives who exhibit leadership qualities with a zeal to
stay ahead in a competitive business environment.
How many years of experience do applicants need
to be admitted?
Applicants need a minimum of 5
years of
experience to apply for the programme. In case any applicant has lesser work
experience,

stay ahead in a competitive business environment.
How many years of experience do applicants need
to be admitted?
Applicants need a minimum of 5
years of
experience to apply for the programme. In case any applicant has lesser work
experience,
feel free to reach out to the admissions team who will evaluate their profile and
advise
accordingly.
Is there an age limit for entry?
There is no age limit for entry.
However,
the programme is more suitable for candidates with experience of at least 5 years.
If they
have less work experience, we encourage them to get in touch with the admissions
team who
will evaluate their profile and advise them accordingly.
We are looking for a candidate who
exhibits strong leadership performance and potential, makes the best use of
opportunities
available, and is motivated to create an impact.
Do we need to write a GMAT, GRE, or CAT to enrol
in this programme?
No, this programme doesn’t require
GMAT,
GRE, or CAT scores.
Can applicants get their profile evaluated?
Feel free to connect with the
team and our counsellors to discuss fitment with the programme. Applicants are
welcome to
send their CV to
and initiate a
conversation with
counsellors for the same.
Are all candidates interviewed?
Post the application process, the
shortlisted candidates are invited to an online interview with our admissions panel.
If an application has been unsuccessful, may
they reapply in the next round?
No, they cannot reapply in the
next round.
Unsuccessful applicants can reapply for the next intake, next year. Re-applicants
requested to submit a new online application unless otherwise indicated.
Please exercise judgement in deciding if they have made any significant changes in
their new
profile since their first application, that would make a significant difference to
their
admissions chances.
How strict are the application deadlines?
Our deadlines are non-negotiable.
Applications must be completed and submitted by midnight to be included in the
ongoing

their new
profile since their first application, that would make a significant difference to
their
admissions chances.
How strict are the application deadlines?
Our deadlines are non-negotiable.
Applications must be completed and submitted by midnight to be included in the
ongoing
round. A complete application includes the online form, all supporting documents,
references, essays, and payment of the application fees.
How will applicants be graded for the course?
All participants will be graded
through a
combination of quizzes, exams, group assignments, class engagement, projects,
simulations,
reflections, etc. The faculty will decide the specific grading matrix for each
course.
Can applicants tailor their learning journey at
PGP Rise?
We offer 16 electives, empowering
students
to take charge of their time and construct a relevant programme that is aligned with
interests and career goals. We have new-age, core, and industry concentrations so
they can
choose to pursue a generalist path toward management or specialise in particular
areas by
choosing industry concentrations. However, regardless of the path students decide to
take
during their elective terms, everybody graduates with the same PGP Rise certificate
with an
opportunity to choose industry concentrations.
Are any of the rounds more or less competitive
than others?
Each round is equally competitive.
acceptance ratio is the same for all rounds. Most importantly, applicants should
dedicate
enough time and thought to their application so that it accurately represents their
strengths and talents.
However, if scholarships are a priority, we encourage applicants to apply in Round
How should applicants approach the essay
section?
The essay section in the
application
section requires careful consideration and thought. We’re looking for clarity,
conciseness,
and cohesion in arguments of applicants as well as in the structure of their essay.
Most

How should applicants approach the essay
section?
The essay section in the
application
section requires careful consideration and thought. We’re looking for clarity,
conciseness,
and cohesion in arguments of applicants as well as in the structure of their essay.
Most
importantly, we encourage them to bring out their most authentic self through these
essays
to give us the best understanding of their thought process, critical thinking, and
communication skills.
Are the fees refunded in case applicants decide
to drop out of the programme?
Fees once submitted are not
eligible for
refunds.
Masters’ Union reserves the right to amend this Refund Policy at any time without
prior
notice.
Can applicants defer my admission offer from
Masters' Union to the next year?
Currently, we are not offering the
option
to defer their admission offer. Those who cannot join the programme in the current
year are
advised to apply again for the next year.

How does the curriculum ensure applicants are equipped
with practical business knowledge?
The programme blends traditional classroom
learning
with experiential "Outclasses" such as building an e-commerce store or growing a
startup from the ground up. This hands-on approach ensures learners not only
gain theoretical knowledge but also acquire the practical skills to build and
manage businesses.
Besides coaching from faculty, who else do we have access
PGP Rise: General Management (Global) offers
exclusive access to a network of
industry leaders including CXOs, MDs, and successful entrepreneurs. These
interactions provide invaluable insights into industry trends, leadership

Besides coaching from faculty, who else do we have access
PGP Rise: General Management (Global) offers
exclusive access to a network of
industry leaders including CXOs, MDs, and successful entrepreneurs. These
interactions provide invaluable insights into industry trends, leadership
strategies, and innovative business practices.
How does the curriculum help develop management skills
that go beyond business knowledge?
Our curriculum goes beyond mastering the
essentials of business and ventures into leadership & self-development and
learning relevant business softwares. This balanced approach ensures that
graduates are not only proficient in technical areas but also equipped with the
soft skills needed to lead teams and navigate complex business environments.
What are the additional resources available for career
preparation?
In addition to support from the Career Advisory Team, we also provide access
to an online mentorship tool called Get Prepped, where learners have instant
access to real-time coaching.

is conducted by the Masters who bring decades of expertise. They are closely
involved in mentoring the learners in their preferred domain.
What makes the faculty at PGP Rise: General Management
(Global) stand out compared to
traditional academic instructors?
The faculty at PGP Rise: General Management
(Global) are seasoned industry
leaders who bring real-world knowledge. They shape their teaching around current
trends, best practices and learnings based on their own experience.
How does learning from faculty with practical experience
enhance my leadership and decision-making skills?
Learning from professionals who have led
organisations and driven change provides invaluable perspectives on
decision-making and leadership. Their practical experiences highlight the
nuances of leading teams, driving growth, and managing crises preparing you to
handle similar challenges.

apply only for the next cohort.
Can applicants defer their admission offer to the next

refunds. Masters’ Union reserves the right to amend this Refund Policy at any

Why HR & OS,
Why Now?
The world of work is transforming faster than ever. With AI,
hybrid teams, and global
mobility reshaping organisations, HR has
become a core driver
of business strategy.
India produces
lakh MBAs
, yet
under 5%
specialise in HR
despite growing
demand for leaders skilled in analytics, culture,
and people strategy.
Globally, the
CHRO is now the #2 most
in-demand C-suite role after
, reflecting HR’s
rising
importance.
The Masters’ Union
PGP in Human
Resources & Organisational Systems
(HR&OS)
develops future-ready HR leaders trained in
fundamentals, people
analytics, and real-world organisational design to shape the future of work.

Requirements
Fill out your details
You will need:
Your latest resume - Applicants are allowed to choose their
own format (needs to be clear and concise). Feel
free to use our
sample resume format.
Your qualification documents - Upload all documents related
to your work experience, academics and
extra-curriculars to support your application.
Upload your CAT/GMAT/GMAT Focus score
You may submit your
CAT/GMAT/GMAT Focus scores in your application.
MU-BAAT
(Masters’ Union Business Aptitude & Admissions Test)
About the Online Scholarship Test
Applicants who do not have a valid CAT or GMAT score (or if you feel
your score does not reflect your true
potential) are required to take the
MU-BAAT. If you have submitted a CAT or GMAT
score and are happy
with it, then you are not required to take the MU-BAAT.
The MU-BAAT is designed to evaluate:
Your communication and business aptitude
Clarity of thought and idea structuring
Creative and analytical thinking
You record your responses online and do not need any prior
preparation. Think of the questions as a
conversation with the Masters’ Union team that helps us understand
you beyond your résumé.
Duration:
45-60 minutes
Format:
9 questions divided into 5 sections. The test starts with a simple,
fun question and gradually moves
to questions focused on your story, motivation, creativity, and
analytical thinking. You will receive short
breaks between sections.
Test Window:
After you submit the application and pay the application fee, our
admissions team will evaluate your profile
and invite you to take the MU-BAAT. You will receive a link from us
course: PGP Human Resources and Organisation Strategy

text:
analytical thinking. You will receive short
breaks between sections.
Test Window:
After you submit the application and pay the application fee, our
admissions team will evaluate your profile
and invite you to take the MU-BAAT. You will receive a link from us
typically 1-2 weeks after you submit your
application. You will then have 14 days to take the test.
Sample Questions:
If you could shadow any business leader for one day, who
would it be and what would you aim to learn?
A D2C skincare brand increased their ad spend, but average
order value fell 20%, while new users grew.
What do you think happened?
Schedule In-Person
Marketing Simulation
& Interview
About In-Person Business Simulation and Interview
Following a comprehensive
evaluation, shortlisted applicants will receive an invitation to
proceed to the

The MU Scholarship for Technology Leaders is for candidates who have
demonstrated technology-oriented leadership either as students or in their jobs,
through innovations, patents or research, and are looking to further their career
prospects in the technology domain.
The P. Dwarakanath India-bound Scholarship is directed at
Indian-origin candidates (i.e. PIO card-holders) who wish to engage with the Indian
corporate sector, as well as Indian professionals currently based abroad, who wish
to return to India to pursue world-class education and catapult their careers to
newer heights.
The Pankaj Bansal Scholarship for Young Leaders aims to identify
young professionals who have displayed exceptional drive and leadership potential
through their professional projects, community service and active participation in
student-led committees, clubs, and other initiatives while in college.
Founder’s Bursary for Entrepreneurs
The Founders’ Bursary for Entrepreneurs funds candidates who have had
entrepreneurial experience, with varying levels of success, and wish to gain more
skills and academic perspectives to carry on with their line of work.
The Siblings Scholarship offers financial support to candidates who
have siblings in the current or previous cohorts of Masters’ Union. This scholarship
recognises the continued trust placed in us by families and celebrates their shared
journey within the Masters’ Union community. Awards are offered as partial waivers
of the tuition fee, as decided by the admissions committee.

Frequently Asked
Questions
What is a PG Programme in Human Resources & Organisation Strategy
(HR&OS)?
A 16-month full-time postgraduate programme blending Masters’
Union’s business foundation with hands-on specialisations in human capital, organisational
design, leadership, and people analytics.
What will I learn in the Programme?
Learn to build people strategies, design organisations, and
lead talent in a fast-changing business environment. Learn directly from top faculty, CHROs,
CXOs, and founders through live case studies, mentorship, and industry-led workshops. Our
“learn by doing” curriculum shapes future HR leaders at the intersection of people,
strategy, culture, and analytics.
What is the program Structure?
The curriculum spans 8 terms across 16 months
Terms 1–2 building core business foundations
course: PGP Human Resources and Organisation Strategy

text:
“learn by doing” curriculum shapes future HR leaders at the intersection of people,
strategy, culture, and analytics.
What is the program Structure?
The curriculum spans 8 terms across 16 months
Terms 1–2 building core business foundations
Terms 3–5 mastering human resources & organisation strategy
Term 6-8: Capstone and consulting projects with real organisations
What will be the batch size of the class?
We will limit the batch size to a maximum of 60 students to
ensure personalised learning, close mentorship from masters, and a highly effective
teacher–student ratio.
What is the eligibility criteria?
The PGP in Human Resources & Organisation
Strategy is open to candidates who have completed their undergraduate degree or will
graduate by April 2026 onwards. Ideal applicants include fresh graduates, early-career
professionals (0–5 years), and professionals aspiring to build careers in people, culture,
and organisational leadership.
How do I apply?
Click
(tag the application now link) on our website, complete the online application
form, and our admissions team will reach out to you at the earliest possible time.
Are scholarships available for the PGP HR&OS programme?
Yes, scholarships are available for
candidates with strong leadership potential, prior people management exposure, and
demonstrated impact in organisational or HR-related initiatives
How can I avail the scholarship?
While completing your application, you can
express interest in available scholarships. Shortlisted candidates will be asked to submit
supporting documents or short essays to confirm their eligibility.
If an application has been unsuccessful, may they reapply in the next
round?
No, candidates cannot reapply in the next
round. They may reapply for the next intake in the following academic year by submitting a
new online application. It’s advisable to do so only if there have been significant updates
or improvements to their profile since the previous application.

No, candidates cannot reapply in the next

Will I get to network with Industry leaders?
Yes, you will learn and engage with top
faculties, CHROs, people leaders, founders, and CXOs across diverse industries.
Can I work on my own startup ideas during the programme?
Absolutely. The programme fosters
entrepreneurial thinking and provides ample opportunities to apply classroom and immersion
learnings to your own ventures.
Are the fees refunded in case applicants decide to drop out of the
programme?
Fees once paid are non-refundable.
Masters’ Union reserves the right to modify this refund policy at any time without prior
notice.
Can applicants defer my admission offer from Masters' Union to the
next year?
At present, we do not offer the option to
defer admission. Candidates unable to join the programme this year are encouraged to reapply
for the next intake.

Why HR & OS,
Why Now?
The world of work is transforming faster than ever. With AI,
hybrid teams, and global
mobility reshaping organisations, HR has
become a core driver
of business strategy.
India produces
lakh MBAs
, yet
under 5%
specialise in HR
despite growing
demand for leaders skilled in analytics, culture,
and people strategy.
Globally, the
CHRO is now the #2 most
in-demand C-suite role after
, reflecting HR’s
rising
importance.
The Masters’ Union
PGP in Human
Resources & Organisational Systems
(HR&OS)
develops future-ready HR leaders trained in
fundamentals, people
analytics, and real-world organisational design to shape the future of work.

Our Immersion Programs offer students hands-on exposure to leading business
practices in India and around the world. Structured around thematic tracks, students
choose their focus area and gain industry experience, real-world insights, and
cultural fluency.

real-world
skills that truly stand out.

engagement along with company visits to Schneider Electric, Rungis Market,

Blueprint to a
Standout Application
Your application is more than just a form—it’s your first pitch to the admissions team.
In this student-led video, Yashovardhan, a senior at Masters’ Union, shares the ultimate
blueprint to crafting a stellar application—from structuring essays to avoiding common
pitfalls.

MU-BAAT
(Masters’ Union Business Aptitude & Admissions Test)
About the Online Scholarship Test
Applicants who do not have a valid CAT or GMAT score (or if you feel
your score does not reflect your true
potential) are required to take the
MU-BAAT. If you have submitted a CAT or GMAT
score and are happy
with it, then you are not required to take the MU-BAAT.
The MU-BAAT is designed to evaluate:
Your communication and business aptitude
Clarity of thought and idea structuring
Creative and analytical thinking
You record your responses online and do not need any prior
preparation. Think of the questions as a
conversation with the Masters’ Union team that helps us understand
you beyond your résumé.
Duration:
45-60 minutes
Format:
9 questions divided into 5 sections. The test starts with a simple,
fun question and gradually moves

text:
preparation. Think of the questions as a

you beyond your résumé.
Duration:
45-60 minutes
Format:
9 questions divided into 5 sections. The test starts with a simple,
fun question and gradually moves
to questions focused on your story, motivation, creativity, and
analytical thinking. You will receive short
breaks between sections.
Test Window:
After you submit the application and pay the application fee, our
admissions team will evaluate your profile
and invite you to take the MU-BAAT. You will receive a link from us
typically 1-2 weeks after you submit your
application. You will then have 14 days to take the test.
Sample Questions:
If you could shadow any business leader for one day, who
would it be and what would you aim to learn?
A D2C skincare brand increased their ad spend, but average
order value fell 20%, while new users grew.
What do you think happened?
Schedule In-Person
Marketing Simulation
& Interview
About In-Person Business Simulation and Interview
Following a comprehensive
evaluation, shortlisted applicants will receive an invitation to
proceed to the
interview stage.
If selected, applicants will be informed of the interview
date, venue, and time 3–5 days prior to the interview.
The interviews will be conducted by distinguished faculty
members and seasoned industry leaders.
Admission
Decision
Status of Evaluation
Based on the final decision of the admissions
committee, applicants will be slotted into the
following categories-
Applicants who have been offered admission to Masters’ Union
will receive their decision along with the
financial structure for the upcoming programme.
For applicants who are waitlisted, the admissions committee
will reconvene and promote candidates from
the waitlisted to the accepted pool, on a regular basis.
Please note that we do not maintain a ranked
waitlist. Your admission is dependent on the spots that
might open up later.

text:
Bharat Innovators Scholarship
The Manoj Kohli Merit-based Scholarship extends financial support to
candidates with exceptional academic records and professional accomplishments. These
scholarships are offered as partial waivers of the tuition fee, as decided by the
admissions committee.
The Rajni Thethi Scholarship for Women extends support to deserving
female candidates with impressive academic and/or professional track record, along
with the drive and potential to emerge as an industry leader.
The Chairman’s Diversity Scholarship aims at the inclusion of
exceptional candidates from marginalised and underserved communities from the Indian
subcontinent, such as the Northeast, Ladakh, Bhutan, and the tribal belts of the
Indian hinterland.
The MU Scholarship for Technology Leaders is for candidates who have
demonstrated technology-oriented leadership either as students or in their jobs,
through innovations, patents or research, and are looking to further their career
prospects in the technology domain.
The P. Dwarakanath India-bound Scholarship is directed at
Indian-origin candidates (i.e. PIO card-holders) who wish to engage with the Indian
corporate sector, as well as Indian professionals currently based abroad, who wish
to return to India to pursue world-class education and catapult their careers to
newer heights.
The Pankaj Bansal Scholarship for Young Leaders aims to identify
young professionals who have displayed exceptional drive and leadership potential
through their professional projects, community service and active participation in
student-led committees, clubs, and other initiatives while in college.
Founder’s Bursary for Entrepreneurs
The Founders’ Bursary for Entrepreneurs funds candidates who have had
entrepreneurial experience, with varying levels of success, and wish to gain more
skills and academic perspectives to carry on with their line of work.
The Siblings Scholarship offers financial support to candidates who
course: PGP Human Resources and Organisation Strategy

text:
The Founders’ Bursary for Entrepreneurs funds candidates who have had
entrepreneurial experience, with varying levels of success, and wish to gain more
skills and academic perspectives to carry on with their line of work.
The Siblings Scholarship offers financial support to candidates who
have siblings in the current or previous cohorts of Masters’ Union. This scholarship
recognises the continued trust placed in us by families and celebrates their shared
journey within the Masters’ Union community. Awards are offered as partial waivers
of the tuition fee, as decided by the admissions committee.
The Bharat Innovators Scholarship is aimed at young Indian
entrepreneurs from non-metropolitan towns and villages who have launched a startup
or built a minimum viable product (MVP). This scholarship supports grassroots
innovators looking to scale their ventures while gaining access to world-class
education, mentorship, and a thriving startup ecosystem.
The Manoj Kohli Merit-based Scholarship extends financial support
to candidates with exceptional academic records and professional
accomplishments. These scholarships are offered as partial waivers of the
tuition fee, as decided by the admissions committee.

Frequently Asked
Questions
What is a PG Programme in Human Resources & Organisation Strategy
(HR&OS)?
A 16-month full-time postgraduate programme blending Masters’
Union’s business foundation with hands-on specialisations in human capital, organisational
design, leadership, and people analytics.
What will I learn in the Programme?
Learn to build people strategies, design organisations, and
lead talent in a fast-changing business environment. Learn directly from top faculty, CHROs,
course: PGP Human Resources and Organisation Strategy

text:
design, leadership, and people analytics.
What will I learn in the Programme?
Learn to build people strategies, design organisations, and
lead talent in a fast-changing business environment. Learn directly from top faculty, CHROs,
CXOs, and founders through live case studies, mentorship, and industry-led workshops. Our
“learn by doing” curriculum shapes future HR leaders at the intersection of people,
strategy, culture, and analytics.
What is the program Structure?
The curriculum spans 8 terms across 16 months
Terms 1–2 building core business foundations
Terms 3–5 mastering human resources & organisation strategy
Term 6-8: Capstone and consulting projects with real organisations
What will be the batch size of the class?
We will limit the batch size to a maximum of 60 students to
ensure personalised learning, close mentorship from masters, and a highly effective
teacher–student ratio.
What is the eligibility criteria?
The PGP in Human Resources & Organisation
Strategy is open to candidates who have completed their undergraduate degree or will
graduate by April 2026 onwards. Ideal applicants include fresh graduates, early-career
professionals (0–5 years), and professionals aspiring to build careers in people, culture,
and organisational leadership.
How do I apply?
Click
(tag the application now link) on our website, complete the online application
form, and our admissions team will reach out to you at the earliest possible time.
Are scholarships available for the PGP HR&OS programme?
Yes, scholarships are available for
candidates with strong leadership potential, prior people management exposure, and
demonstrated impact in organisational or HR-related initiatives
How can I avail the scholarship?
While completing your application, you can
express interest in available scholarships. Shortlisted candidates will be asked to submit
supporting documents or short essays to confirm their eligibility.
If an application has been unsuccessful, may they reapply in the next
course: PGP Human Resources and Organisation Strategy

text:
While completing your application, you can
express interest in available scholarships. Shortlisted candidates will be asked to submit
supporting documents or short essays to confirm their eligibility.
If an application has been unsuccessful, may they reapply in the next
round?
No, candidates cannot reapply in the next
round. They may reapply for the next intake in the following academic year by submitting a
new online application. It’s advisable to do so only if there have been significant updates
or improvements to their profile since the previous application.
Will I get to network with Industry leaders?
Yes, you will learn and engage with top
faculties, CHROs, people leaders, founders, and CXOs across diverse industries.
Can I work on my own startup ideas during the programme?
Absolutely. The programme fosters
entrepreneurial thinking and provides ample opportunities to apply classroom and immersion
learnings to your own ventures.
Are the fees refunded in case applicants decide to drop out of the
programme?
Fees once paid are non-refundable.
Masters’ Union reserves the right to modify this refund policy at any time without prior
notice.
Can applicants defer my admission offer from Masters' Union to the
next year?
At present, we do not offer the option to
defer admission. Candidates unable to join the programme this year are encouraged to reapply
for the next intake.

The Business of Sport
Dive into global sports, leagues, teams, events, and athlete brands
while creating real fan-engagement solutions for sports businesses.
Global Sports Ecosystems:
How major leagues, teams, and federations operate across the world.
League & Team Management:
What it takes to run, scale, and manage professional sports teams
and leagues.
Sports Marketing & Sponsorships:
How brands activate, measure, and monetise partnerships in sport.
Athlete Brand Management:
How athletes build, grow, and protect their personal brands.
Course: Venue & Event Operations
How to handle logistics, safety, budgeting, and fan experience for
large sports events.
VIP - MVP:
Transform classroom learning into a live, test-ready product for the
gaming & sports market.
Sports Trek:
Go behind the scenes of leagues, teams, and gaming studios with curated
on-ground sports treks.
Sports Streaming Challenge:
Go inside the world of sports streaming. See how platforms earn, grow
and scale and build your own channel from scratch
Gaming, Media & Technology
Master gaming, esports, digital media, and sports tech through
hands-on content, broadcast, and data projects shaping modern sport.

How to build and monetise games through in-app revenue, ads, and
subscriptions.

Gaming, Media & Technology
Master gaming, esports, digital media, and sports tech through
hands-on content, broadcast, and data projects shaping modern sport.
Gaming Economics & Monetisation:
How to build and monetise games through in-app revenue, ads, and
subscriptions.

communities.
Digital Media & Sports Broadcasting:

and decisions.
VIP - GTM:
Craft a powerful GTM strategy that showcases your edge, ensures smooth

International Immersion:

industry visits, workshops, and live league exposure abroad.
Strategy, Analytics & Leadership
Gain expertise in sports finance, analytics, law, future tech, and
high-performance leadership to make strategic industry decisions.
Sports Finance & Revenue Models:
How to understand valuations, P&Ls, salaries, transfer fees, and
revenue levers.
Data-Driven Decision-Making in Sport:
How to use dashboards, metrics, and models for high-stakes
decisions.
Sports Law, Ethics & Governance:
How to navigate contracts, regulations, athlete rights, and league
governance.
Future of Sports & Gaming:
How AI, AR/VR, wearables, and new tech will reshape the next decade.
Leadership in High-Performance Teams:
How to lead athletes, creators, and sports organisations under
pressure.

ecosystem. From corporates and leagues to studios and stadiums, students gain
real-world insights and industry-ready skills.

Shortlisting &

Aptitude Test
About the Online Scholarship Test
Applicants who do not have a
valid CAT or GMAT score (or if you feel your score does not
reflect
your true
potential) are required to take
the MU-BAAT.
If you have
submitted a
CAT or GMAT score and are happy
with it, then you are not required to take the MU-BAAT.
The MU-BAAT is designed to evaluate:
Your communication and business aptitude
Clarity of thought and idea structuring
Creative and analytical thinking
You record your responses online and do not need any prior
preparation. Think of the questions as a
conversation with the Masters’ Union team that helps us understand
you beyond your résumé.
Duration:
45-60 minutes
Format:
9 questions divided into 5 sections. The test starts with a simple,
fun question and gradually moves
to questions focused on your story, motivation, creativity, and
analytical thinking. You will receive short
breaks between sections.
Test Window:
After you submit the application and pay the application fee, our
admissions team will evaluate your profile
and invite you to take the MU-BAAT. You will receive a link from us
typically 1-2 weeks after you submit your
application. You will then have 14 days to take the test.
Sample Questions:
If you could shadow any business leader for one day, who
would it be and what would you aim to learn?
A D2C skincare brand increased their ad spend, but average
order value fell 20%, while new users grew.
What do you think happened?

& Interview

The Manoj Kohli Merit-based Scholarship extends financial support to
candidates with exceptional academic records and professional accomplishments. These
scholarships are offered as partial waivers of the tuition fee, as decided by the
admissions committee.
The Rajni Thethi Scholarship for Women extends support to deserving
female candidates with impressive academic and/or professional track record, along
with the drive and potential to emerge as an industry leader.
The Chairman’s Diversity Scholarship aims at the inclusion of
exceptional candidates from marginalised and underserved communities from the Indian
subcontinent, such as the Northeast, Ladakh, Bhutan, and the tribal belts of the
Indian hinterland.
The MU Scholarship for Technology Leaders is for candidates who have
demonstrated technology-oriented leadership either as students or in their jobs,
through innovations, patents or research, and are looking to further their career
prospects in the technology domain.
The P. Dwarakanath India-bound Scholarship is directed at
Indian-origin candidates (i.e. PIO card-holders) who wish to engage with the Indian
corporate sector, as well as Indian professionals currently based abroad, who wish
to return to India to pursue world-class education and catapult their careers to
newer heights.

The Pankaj Bansal Scholarship for Young Leaders aims to identify
young professionals who have displayed exceptional drive and leadership potential
through their professional projects, community service and active participation in
student-led committees, clubs, and other initiatives while in college.
Founder’s Bursary for Entrepreneurs
The Founders’ Bursary for Entrepreneurs funds candidates who have had
entrepreneurial experience, with varying levels of success, and wish to gain more
skills and academic perspectives to carry on with their line of work.
The Siblings Scholarship offers financial support to candidates who
have siblings in the current or previous cohorts of Masters’ Union. This scholarship
recognises the continued trust placed in us by families and celebrates their shared
journey within the Masters’ Union community. Awards are offered as partial waivers
of the tuition fee, as decided by the admissions committee.
The Bharat Innovators Scholarship is aimed at young Indian
entrepreneurs from non-metropolitan towns and villages who have launched a startup
or built a minimum viable product (MVP). This scholarship supports grassroots
innovators looking to scale their ventures while gaining access to world-class
education, mentorship, and a thriving startup ecosystem.

Frequently Asked
Questions
What is a PG Programme in Sports Management & Gaming (SMG)?
A 16-month full-time postgraduate programme blending Masters’
Union’s business foundation with hands-on specialisations in sports management, e-sports,
media, and analytics.
What will I learn in the programme?
Learn to run sports leagues, manage athletes, and build gaming
startups in a rapidly evolving sports and digital ecosystem. Learn directly from top
faculty, CXOs, ex-athletes, and sports entrepreneurs through live case studies, mentorship,
and industry-led workshops. Our “learn by doing” curriculum shapes future leaders at the
intersection of sports, analytics, media, and entrepreneurship.
What is the programme structure?
The curriculum spans 6 terms across 16 months
Terms 1-2: building business fundamentals
Terms 3-5: mastering industry-specific skills

intersection of sports, analytics, media, and entrepreneurship.
What is the programme structure?
The curriculum spans 6 terms across 16 months
Terms 1-2: building business fundamentals
Terms 3-5: mastering industry-specific skills
Term 6: Live project with a sports or gaming organisation
What will be the batch size of the class?
We will limit the batch size to a maximum of 60 students to
maximize individual attention from masters, and have an impactful teacher-student ratio.
What is the eligibility criteria?
The PGP in Sports Management & Gaming is
open to candidates who have completed their undergraduate degree or will graduate by April
2026 onwards. Ideal applicants include fresh graduates, early-career professionals (0-5
years), and entrepreneurs in sports tech or e-sports.
How do I apply?
Click
(tag the application now link) on our website, complete the online application
form, and our admissions team will reach out to you at the earliest possible time.
Are scholarships available for the PGP SMG programme?
Yes available for the Sports enthusiasts &
athletes and Sports tech & e-sports entrepreneurs.
How can we avail the Scholarship?
While completing your application, you can
express interest in available scholarships. Shortlisted candidates will be asked to submit
supporting documents or short essays to confirm their eligibility.
If an application has been unsuccessful, may they reapply in the next
round?
No, candidates cannot reapply in the next
round. They may reapply for the next intake in the following academic year by submitting a
new online application. It’s advisable to do so only if there have been significant updates
or improvements to their profile since the previous application.
Will I get to network with Industry Leaders?
Yes you will learn and engage with our top
faculties, sports & gaming founders and CXO's, ex-athletes.
Can I work on my own startup ideas during the programme?
Absolutely. The programme fosters

Will I get to network with Industry Leaders?
Yes you will learn and engage with our top
faculties, sports & gaming founders and CXO's, ex-athletes.
Can I work on my own startup ideas during the programme?
Absolutely. The programme fosters
entrepreneurial thinking and provides ample opportunities to apply classroom and immersion
learnings to your own ventures.
Are classes online or in-person?
The programme follows a blended (hybrid)
format, with key sessions and immersions held in person. Certain In-Class and Out-Class
modules such as workshops, challenges, and mentor sessions may be conducted in hybrid mode
to offer greater flexibility.
Are the fees refunded in case applicants decide to drop out of the
programme?
Fees once paid are non-refundable.
Masters’ Union reserves the right to modify this refund policy at any time without prior
notice.
Can applicants defer my admission offer from Masters' Union to the
next year?
At present, we do not offer the option to
defer admission. Candidates unable to join the programme this year are encouraged to reapply
for the next intake.

Aptitude Test
About the Online Scholarship Test
Applicants who do not have a
valid CAT or GMAT score (or if you feel your score does not
reflect
your true
potential) are required to take
the MU-BAAT.
If you have
submitted a
CAT or GMAT score and are happy
with it, then you are not required to take the MU-BAAT.
The MU-BAAT is designed to evaluate:
Your communication and business aptitude
Clarity of thought and idea structuring
Creative and analytical thinking
You record your responses online and do not need any prior
preparation. Think of the questions as a
conversation with the Masters’ Union team that helps us understand
you beyond your résumé.
Duration:
45-60 minutes
Format:
9 questions divided into 5 sections. The test starts with a simple,
fun question and gradually moves
to questions focused on your story, motivation, creativity, and
analytical thinking. You will receive short
breaks between sections.
Test Window:
After you submit the application and pay the application fee, our
admissions team will evaluate your profile
and invite you to take the MU-BAAT. You will receive a link from us
typically 1-2 weeks after you submit your
application. You will then have 14 days to take the test.
Sample Questions:
If you could shadow any business leader for one day, who
would it be and what would you aim to learn?
A D2C skincare brand increased their ad spend, but average
order value fell 20%, while new users grew.
What do you think happened?

The Manoj Kohli Merit-based Scholarship extends financial support to
candidates with exceptional academic records and professional accomplishments. These
scholarships are offered as partial waivers of the tuition fee, as decided by the
admissions committee.
The Rajni Thethi Scholarship for Women extends support to deserving
female candidates with impressive academic and/or professional track record, along
with the drive and potential to emerge as an industry leader.
The Chairman’s Diversity Scholarship aims at the inclusion of
exceptional candidates from marginalised and underserved communities from the Indian
subcontinent, such as the Northeast, Ladakh, Bhutan, and the tribal belts of the
Indian hinterland.
The MU Scholarship for Technology Leaders is for candidates who have
demonstrated technology-oriented leadership either as students or in their jobs,
through innovations, patents or research, and are looking to further their career
prospects in the technology domain.
The P. Dwarakanath India-bound Scholarship is directed at
Indian-origin candidates (i.e. PIO card-holders) who wish to engage with the Indian

through innovations, patents or research, and are looking to further their career
prospects in the technology domain.
The P. Dwarakanath India-bound Scholarship is directed at
Indian-origin candidates (i.e. PIO card-holders) who wish to engage with the Indian
corporate sector, as well as Indian professionals currently based abroad, who wish
to return to India to pursue world-class education and catapult their careers to
newer heights.
The Pankaj Bansal Scholarship for Young Leaders aims to identify
young professionals who have displayed exceptional drive and leadership potential
through their professional projects, community service and active participation in
student-led committees, clubs, and other initiatives while in college.
Founder’s Bursary for Entrepreneurs
The Founders’ Bursary for Entrepreneurs funds candidates who have had
entrepreneurial experience, with varying levels of success, and wish to gain more
skills and academic perspectives to carry on with their line of work.
The Siblings Scholarship offers financial support to candidates who
have siblings in the current or previous cohorts of Masters’ Union. This scholarship
recognises the continued trust placed in us by families and celebrates their shared
journey within the Masters’ Union community. Awards are offered as partial waivers
of the tuition fee, as decided by the admissions committee.
The Bharat Innovators Scholarship is aimed at young Indian
entrepreneurs from non-metropolitan towns and villages who have launched a startup
or built a minimum viable product (MVP). This scholarship supports grassroots
innovators looking to scale their ventures while gaining access to world-class
education, mentorship, and a thriving startup ecosystem.

Frequently Asked
Questions
What is a PG Programme in Sports Management & Gaming (SMG)?
A 16-month full-time postgraduate programme blending Masters’
Union’s business foundation with hands-on specialisations in sports management, e-sports,
media, and analytics.
What will I learn in the programme?
Learn to run sports leagues, manage athletes, and build gaming
startups in a rapidly evolving sports and digital ecosystem. Learn directly from top
faculty, CXOs, ex-athletes, and sports entrepreneurs through live case studies, mentorship,
and industry-led workshops. Our “learn by doing” curriculum shapes future leaders at the

startups in a rapidly evolving sports and digital ecosystem. Learn directly from top
faculty, CXOs, ex-athletes, and sports entrepreneurs through live case studies, mentorship,
and industry-led workshops. Our “learn by doing” curriculum shapes future leaders at the
intersection of sports, analytics, media, and entrepreneurship.
What is the programme structure?
The curriculum spans 6 terms across 16 months
Terms 1-2: building business fundamentals
Terms 3-5: mastering industry-specific skills
Term 6: Live project with a sports or gaming organisation
What will be the batch size of the class?
We will limit the batch size to a maximum of 60 students to
maximize individual attention from masters, and have an impactful teacher-student ratio.
What is the eligibility criteria?
The PGP in Sports Management & Gaming is
open to candidates who have completed their undergraduate degree or will graduate by April
2026 onwards. Ideal applicants include fresh graduates, early-career professionals (0-5
years), and entrepreneurs in sports tech or e-sports.
How do I apply?
Click
(tag the application now link) on our website, complete the online application
form, and our admissions team will reach out to you at the earliest possible time.
Are scholarships available for the PGP SMG programme?
Yes available for the Sports enthusiasts &
athletes and Sports tech & e-sports entrepreneurs.
How can we avail the Scholarship?
While completing your application, you can
express interest in available scholarships. Shortlisted candidates will be asked to submit
supporting documents or short essays to confirm their eligibility.
If an application has been unsuccessful, may they reapply in the next
round?
No, candidates cannot reapply in the next
round. They may reapply for the next intake in the following academic year by submitting a
new online application. It’s advisable to do so only if there have been significant updates
or improvements to their profile since the previous application.

CATEGORY: Admissions
SOURCE: MastersUnion Official

HOW TO APPLY:
1. Visit the portal and fill in the comprehensive application form.
2. Upload academic transcripts from class 10, 12, and Graduation.
3. Submit your MU-BAAT test score or equivalent (CAT/GMAT/GRE).
4. Provide the video/text essay prompt responses.

REQUIREMENTS & SELECTION CRITERIA:
- Intellectual Readiness: Evaluated through test scores and academic consistency.
- Leadership Potential: Demonstrated through professional achievements or extracurricular initiatives.
- Communication: Assessed via the video essay and the personal interview round.
- Diversity: The cohort is selected to ensure a mix of engineers, entrepreneurs, and liberal arts students.

DATES: 
Applications for the upcoming cohort close on the last Sunday of each round (typically January, March, and May).

Experience from Day One
Over 80 students have already interned at top brands,
EY, The Indian Express, Chaayos, Indigo,
